methods.description: This method involves the comparison and analysis of rhinocerotid fossils from the Negev and other regions, using both direct observations and published materials. The purpose is to classify and understand the characteristics of early and early middle Miocene rhinocerotids.

steps.description: The fragile rhinocerotid remains from the Negev are curated and housed at the National Natural History Collections of The Hebrew University of Jerusalem (Israel).
steps.searchable_summary: Access rhinocerotid fossil remains from the National Natural History Collections of The Hebrew University of Jerusalem

steps.description: We base the comparisons on direct observations of the fossils, as well as published material on early and early middle Miocene rhinocerotids.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare fossils using direct observations and literature review

steps.description: We consulted various published works for additional data on rhinocerotids.
steps.searchable_summary: Consult published works for rhinocerotid data

steps.description: We made direct observations on juvenile mandibles and teeth and postcranial remains of various rhinocerotid species from different locations.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and analyze juvenile mandibles, teeth, and postcranial remains

steps.description: Classification above genus level follows Antoine et al. (2010).
steps.searchable_summary: Classify fossils above genus level using Antoine et al. (2010)

steps.description: Description of postcranial remains is based on Guérin (1980) and Antoine (2002).
steps.searchable_summary: Describe postcranial remains using Guérin (1980) and Antoine (2002)
